title: Beautiful Drone Footage of a Cloud Forest #cloudforest #monteverde #costarica #shorts #drone #nature
creator: Our Backman Family
notes: CANDIDATE-SOURCED, INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED THIS SESSION -- one exemplar added to the forest/jungle-canopy landscape subgenre bucket. A prior agent's candidate JSON proposed this URL (https://www.youtube.com/shorts/f5SMVU7VQu4); per this task's step 1 requirement it was NOT trusted verbatim -- it was independently re-fetched live in this session via YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/f5SMVU7VQu4&format=json), which returned title='Beautiful Drone Footage of a Cloud Forest #cloudforest #monteverde #costarica #shorts #drone #nature', author_name='Our Backman Family', author_url='https://www.youtube.com/@OurBackmanFamily' -- an exact match on title and creator/channel to the candidate record, confirming this session's own independent verification agrees with the prior agent's finding. A follow-up direct curl fetch of the live watch page (https://www.youtube.com/shorts/f5SMVU7VQu4, HTTP 200, ~1.39MB HTML, desktop User-Agent) was grepped case-insensitively for 'creative commons'/'creativeCommons'/'licenseType' and found zero matches, corroborating the candidate's 'no CC marking found' conclusion via an independent method (page-source grep rather than the candidate's own unspecified method). A separate WebFetch attempt against the same URL for description/drone-model/editing-style text returned only a truncated-content notice with no extractable body, so no additional editorial signal was obtained from that call in this session. Two WebSearch queries were run this session: 'Our Backman Family Monteverde cloud forest drone footage' surfaced only this same Short (confirming discoverability but no independent writeup of its cut rhythm, shot length, or transitions) plus unrelated stock-footage/tourism-info results (Getty Images, Shutterstock, Wikipedia's Monteverde Cloud Forest Reserve page, TripAdvisor); 'Our Backman Family youtube channel drone Costa Rica' returned a one-line channel description characterizing 'Our Backman Family' as inviting viewers to 'join them on their daily adventures' -- i.e. a family travel-vlog channel, not a dedicated aerial-cinematography or drone-specialist outlet -- alongside several unrelated dedicated Costa-Rica-drone channels (Drone Costa Rica, Costa Rica Drone Trips, Drone Studios) that are NOT this creator and are not conflated with it here. license_category is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': determination method is identical to every other record in this pack -- absence of any explicit Creative Commons marking on the fetched page (confirmed independently this session via the grep above) is treated as insufficient evidence for a permissive license, per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly and explicitly confirmed otherwise). Consequently local_media_path stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo. duration is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response for this Short returns only height/width/thumbnail/html-embed fields, no duration field, and no YouTube Data API call was made in this session -- rather than fabricate a plausible-looking number (YouTube Shorts are conventionally under 60s, but this session did not independently verify this specific upload's runtime), this field is left unset pending a real API call or manual playback confirmation (see reference_pack/README.md's refresh/expiry process). award_or_showcase_provenance is left null: this is ordinary family-travel-vlog content with a hashtag-dense Shorts title -- no documented contest placement, festival selection, or brand/publisher feature was found or claimed by either this session's searches or the prior agent's -- inventing one would violate this task's explicit instruction not to fabricate provenance for reel-style content with no formal award. scores (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are scores_provenance='manually_estimated' placeholder judgments, deliberately calibrated at the lower end of this pack's documented range: unlike valsoiapg-gran-sasso-alpine.json (0.75/0.72/0.76, which at least documents a named flagship drone -- DJI Mavic 4 Pro -- and tags DJI's own official accounts) or the contest-winning exemplars (skypixel-landscape.json 0.78/0.75/0.82, skypixel11_africa_unseen_ellisvanjason.json 0.8/0.8/0.85), this record has no documented drone model, no stated resolution/bitrate, no follower/engagement count, no contest placement, and its own channel is independently characterized (via this session's WebSearch) as a general family-adventure vlog rather than a dedicated aerial-cinematography outlet -- all polish-signal absences, not merely 'unverified', so 0.72/0.70/0.72 (below valsoiapg's 0.75/0.72/0.76) is used rather than matching or exceeding that record. These are NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py), since no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the spec's no-bulk-download constraint. composite_score IS a real computation: it was produced this session by calling this repo's own drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.72, exposure=0.70, motion_smoothness=0.72, composition=None) and drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights() (which this session confirmed returns weights_version='default-v2': sharpness=exposure=motion_smoothness=composition=0.25, the current Milestone-2 default per weights.py) via '.venv/bin/python3' in this session. Because composition's score is still None here (this pack's Milestone-1 Capability-1 convention, retained per this task's instructions), compute_composite_score's defensive 'skip any signal whose score is None' behavior excludes composition from both the weighted sum and the renormalization denominator regardless of its nonzero 0.25 weight, so the result reduces to the equal-thirds mean of the three supplied scores: (0.72+0.70+0.72)/3 = 0.7133333333333333 (rounded to 0.7133 here), confirmed by actually running the function this session rather than hand-computed. The composite arithmetic itself is genuine and reproducible even though its three inputs are estimates rather than measurements.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: Not reviewed via live playback: no browser automation tool was invoked in this session for this candidate, so no scrubbing/playback of the actual video was performed -- review_method stays 'not_reviewed', consistent with the prior editorial-review agent's finding. Text-provenance attempts this session were: (1) a WebFetch of the source URL itself, which returned only a truncated-content notice with no extractable description/body text; (2) a WebSearch for 'Our Backman Family Monteverde cloud forest drone footage', which surfaced only this same Short (title already captured above) plus unrelated Monteverde tourism/stock-footage pages, none describing this video's cut rhythm, shot count, shot length, or transition style; (3) a WebSearch for 'Our Backman Family youtube channel drone Costa Rica', which surfaced only a generic one-line channel description ('join them on their daily adventures') with no reel-specific editorial detail. No field below is populated from a guess, per the schema's honesty contract -- format/estimated_cut_count/avg_shot_length_seconds/transition_styles_observed all stay null/empty.
